title: Unit dictionary
description: Benchling unit types and the units that belong to them.

items.title: Unit type and its units
items.description: A dimensional unit type (e.g., Mass, Volume, Mass concentration) and its units.

Unit type.description: Unit type name (e.g., Mass, Volume, Mass concentration).

Description.description: Documentation description for the unit type (not saved to Benchling).

Allow as container quantity.description: Controls whether this unit type appears in the dropdown of selectable units for a Container schema's built-in Quantity field. Optional. Defaults to false if omitted.

Allow as concentration.description: Controls whether this unit type appears in the dropdown of selectable units for a Container schema's built-in Concentration field. Optional. Defaults to false if omitted.

Formula.description: Defines this unit type as derived from other unit types (e.g., Mass concentration = Mass / Volume). Omit for base/simple unit types (e.g., pH, Percent) that have no formula. Mirrors the Benchling UI, which allows at most 2 numerators and at most 1 denominator. Numerator/Denominator terms reference the Name (not the Symbol) of a unit defined elsewhere in this Unit_dictionary; matching is case-sensitive.

Numerators.description: Up to 2 unit names (from any unit type's dictionary) multiplied together in the numerator.

items.description: A unit Name (not Symbol), optionally raised to an integer power (Power defaults to 1 and is normally omitted). A Power other than 1 only occurs on a small number of Benchling built-in unit types (e.g., Volume = decimeter^3) and is not expected to be hand-authored.

Denominator.description: A single unit Name (not Symbol) dividing the numerator(s), optionally raised to an integer power (Power defaults to 1 and is normally omitted). Omit if there is no denominator.

API ID.description: API ID for the unit type created during an export operation. This is ignored for import operations.

Dimensional terms (do not hand-edit).description: Opaque, Benchling-internal dimensional signature for this unit type (e.g., MASS^1, LENGTH^-3). This is not shown anywhere in the Benchling UI, is not reliably derivable from Formula (Benchling's own data is not always internally consistent here), and has no meaningful hand-authored value. Populated verbatim by export tooling; carried through unmodified on import. Omit for hand-authored unit types (import will proceed without it).

Dimension.description: Benchling's dimension token (e.g., MASS, LENGTH, TIME, AMOUNT), verbatim from the export.

Units.description: Units belonging to this unit type. Exactly one must be the base unit (Conversion factor: Base unit).

items.title: Unit
items.description: A single unit belonging to the enclosing unit type.

Description.description: Documentation description for the unit (not saved to Benchling).

Symbol.description: Unit symbol as displayed in Benchling (e.g., mg, mL).

Conversion factor.description: Multiplicative factor to convert a value in this unit to the unit type's base unit. Exactly one unit per unit type must be the literal string 'Base unit'; all others are a decimal number expressed as a string.

Aliases.description: Alternate symbols/spellings for the unit (e.g., unicode variants like µg).

SI name.description: SI base unit name (e.g., METER, SECOND), if applicable. Rare.

API ID.description: API ID for the unit created during an export operation. This is ignored for import operations.
